channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: Owen Michaels scored two third-period goals to seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four championship in a 6-2 victory over Boston University on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: John Wawrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fan reaches into Mike Trout's glove to snatch a catch from Angels outfielder
item.description: A fan snatched the ball out of Mike Trout’s glove after the Los Angeles Angels star reached into the right field stands to make what appeared to be a great catch.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Patrick Reed and Ludvig Aberg make Saturday moves at the Masters. Justin Rose drops back
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Patrick Reed says his putter has been “on vacation.” Golf fans who primarily watch major championships and the PGA Tour might have the impression that Reed has been lounging on a beach somewhere, too.
item.dc:creator: BEN NUCKOLS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Gunner Stockton strengthens hold on Georgia starting quarterback job with strong spring practice
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Carson Beck's decision to transfer to Miami opened the door for Gunner Stockton to take over as Georgia's starting quarterback.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Piastri on pole and Norris only sixth in qualifying for F1's Bahrain Grand Prix
item.description: Oscar Piastri took pole in qualifying for the Bahrain Grand Prix on Saturday as his McLaren teammate and Formula 1 standings leader Lando Norris could only manage sixth. George Russell was a surprisingly strong challenger and placed second for Mercedes, .168 of a second off Piastri's time.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ELLINGWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Belichick reveals massive Patriots regret he made twice, in new book
item.description: Bill Belichick revealed mistakes he made during his illustrious New England Patriots tenure, and among them was passing on Lamar Jackson twice in the 2018 NFL Draft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zach Johnson was feeling nostalgic. The former Masters champ shot 66, his best at Augusta National
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Zach Johnson was feeling nostalgic after his third round at the Masters on Saturday. Probably felt a bit like the old days while he was playing it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Masters marker creates a buzz on social media from golf fans wishing they could take his place
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Michael McDermott became the envy of the golf world — again. For the second time in three years, McDermott served as a non-competing marker at the Masters, this time playing alongside Tim Kim for 18 holes in the third round at Augusta National on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: De Bruyne sparks Man City comeback win as Arsenal and Forest stumble in Premier League
item.description: Kevin De Bruyne wants to deliver Champions League qualification to Manchester City as a parting gift to his club of the last 10 years. The Belgium playmaker certainly looked like a man on a mission in sparking City's remarkable fightback against Crystal Palace in the Premier League on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scottie Scheffler gets a break from an azalea, escapes a magnolia and trails by 3 at the Masters
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Even the azaleas at Augusta National seem to love Scottie Scheffler. The magnolia trees weren't so friendly, though.
item.dc:creator: BEN NUCKOLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MLB umpire called 'perfect game' in Royals win over Twins, metrics tracking shows
item.description: The Royals defeated the Twins on Thursday. Umpire Scorecards shined a light on MLB umpire Mark Ripperger for what they concluded was a perfectly called game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prep roundup: Jasir Rencher flips commitment to Washington from Texas A&M, Livermore Stampede rolls on
item.description: Bay Area softball teams battle at Livermore Stampede. Jasir Rencher flips commitment after coaching change. Serra, St. Francis, Leigh, Menlo-Atherton, De La Salle, Bellarmine, St. Ignatius baseball in action.
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Dodgers host the Cubs on 7-game home win streak
item.description: Chicago Cubs (9-7, second in the NL Central) vs. Los Angeles Dodgers (11-4, third in the NL West) Los Angeles; Saturday, 9:10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tom Brady flashes legendary arm for fans at grand opening of new store; discusses Joe Montana, Super Bowl win
item.description: NFL legend Tom Brady opened up a CardVault by Tom Brady store at the American Dream and offered fans a treat by throwing footballs to them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Warriors control play-in destiny after handling Blazers
item.description: Golden State Warriors survive Curry injury scare, can now clinch Top 6 seed with victory over Clippers on Sunday
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Cardinals reserve C Yohel Pozo making the most of his first trip back to the majors since 2021
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Reserve catcher Yohel Pozo will remember his first time being in St. Louis. Pozo came off the bench in the second inning against the Philadelphia Phillies on Friday night after teammate Masyn Winn left after one inning with lower back spasms, sliding into the shortstop's No.
item.dc:creator: WARREN MAYES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Local golf club students attend The Masters for first time
item.description: It was a beautiful Friday for the second round of The Masters, and some middle schoolers got to attend the tournament for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Brad Means, Fox 5

item.title: Blackhawks host the Jets following Foligno's 2-goal game
item.description: Winnipeg Jets (54-21-4, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (23-46-10, in the Central Division) Chicago; Saturday, 7 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks host the Winnipeg Jets after Nick Foligno's two-goal game against the Boston Bruins in the Blackhawks' 5-2 win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox enter matchup with the Red Sox on losing streak
item.description: Boston Red Sox (7-7, third in the AL East) vs. Chicago White Sox (2-10, fifth in the AL Central) Chicago; Friday, 7:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Erik Karlsson scores his 200th goal as the Penguins beat the Blackhawks 5-0
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Erik Karlsson scored his 200th career goal to help the Pittsburgh Penguins beat the Chicago Blackhawks 5-0 on Tuesday night. Sidney Crosby and Kris Letang also scored for Pittsburgh, which lost 3-1 at Chicago on Sunday. Kevin Hayes added two goals in the third period.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Nolan Jones draws walk with bases-loaded to give Guardians 1-0 victory over White Sox in home opener
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Nolan Jones drew a bases-loaded walk from Mike Clevinger in the ninth inning, and the Cleveland Guardians won their home opener 1-0 over the Chicago White Sox on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jaquan Brisker's bond with Bears' DC Dennis Allen predates his NFL career
item.description: Jaquan Brisker was originally drafted by the Saints, but instead joined the Bears, where he will be reunited with his former defensive coordinator, Dennis Allen, who is now the Bears' defensive coordinator.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Keyonte George scores career-high 35 in Jazz loss
item.description: ATLANTA (ABC4 Sports) - Keyonte George set a new career high with 35 points, but it wasn't enough to stop the Utah Jazz' nine-game losing streak in a 147-135 loss to the Atlanta Hawks Sunday. The Jazz have lost 19 of 20 overall. Utah has given up at least 140 points in three straight games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Coby White scores 37, Josh Giddey adds 23 as Bulls beat Hornets 131-117 for 3rd straight win
item.description: Coby White scored 37 points, shooting 6 of 8 from 3-point range, Josh Giddey added 23 points, 10 rebounds and eight assists and the Chicago Bulls rolled past the Charlotte Hornets 131-117 on Sunday for their third straight win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
